author: nflaig

author: nflaig

author: potuz

author: nflaig
content:
I am happy to have figured it out so fine either way at least I know now why prysm votes correctly, need to make `deathstar` bit smarter to not pack the evidence into the block it proposes

but locally prysm vote counts are all over the place in a small devnet with low validator count as it depends on who is the proposer, which is super confusing if you look the the v2 fork choice dumps

author: potuz
content: Thanks for looking into this so detailed

author: nflaig
content:
simple reproduction locally
```yaml
participants_matrix:
  el:
    - el_type: nethermind
      el_image: ethpandaops/nethermind:glamsterdam-devnet-5
  cl:
    - cl_type: lodestar
      cl_image: ethpandaops/lodestar:deathstar # <-- reorgs timely payloads, no longer packs PTC attestations
    - cl_type: lodestar
      cl_image: ethpandaops/lodestar:glamsterdam-devnet-5-699d0f4 # <-- faulty lodestar that supports deathstar
    - cl_type: lodestar
      cl_image: ethpandaops/lodestar:glamsterdam-devnet-5 # <-- lodestar that works per spec
    - cl_type: prysm
      cl_image: ethpandaops/prysm-beacon-chain:glamsterdam-devnet-5 # <-- prysm supports deathstar blocks
network_params:
  preset: mainnet
  gloas_fork_epoch: 1

global_log_level: info

additional_services:
  - dora
```

author: nflaig
content: <@412614104222531604> if you up early, can we roll out `deathstar` again? I pushed some more changes, namely that it will no longer pack ptc attestations that vote against it's decision to reorg the timely payload
